title: Road transport drivers n.e.c.
description: Attend to live farm, ranch, open range or aquacultural animals that may include cattle, sheep, swine, goats, horses and other equines, poultry, rabbits, finfish, shellfish, and bees. Attend to animals produced for animal products, such as meat, fur, skins, feathers, eggs, milk, and honey. Duties may include feeding, watering, herding, grazing, milking, castrating, branding, de-beaking, weighing, catching, and loading animals. May maintain records on animals; examine animals to detect diseases and injuries; assist in birth deliveries; and administer medications, vaccinations, or insecticides as appropriate. May clean and maintain animal housing areas. Includes workers who shear wool from sheep and collect eggs in hatcheries. All agricultural workers not listed separately. Inspect, test, sort, sample, or weigh nonagricultural raw materials or processed, machined, fabricated, or assembled parts or products for defects, wear, and deviations from specifications. May use precision measuring instruments and complex test equipment. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Order food for animals and arrange for its delivery.
Protect herds from predators, using trained dogs.
Groom, clip, trim, or shear animals.
Maintain growth, feeding, production, and cost records.
Patrol grazing lands on horseback or using all-terrain vehicles.
Segregate animals according to weight, age, color, and physical condition.
Herd livestock to pastures or to scales and trucks.
Mark livestock to identify ownership and grade using brands, tags, or tattoos.
Clean stalls, pens, and equipment using disinfectant solutions and brushes.
Move livestock from one location to another, manually or using vehicles.
Inspect, maintain, and repair equipment and buildings related to animal care.
Administer medical treatment and arrange for veterinary services.
Examine animals to detect illness, injury, or disease.
Drive trucks or tractors to distribute feed to animals.
Feed and water livestock and monitor food and water supplies.
Shift animals between grazing areas to ensure proper access to food.
Perform minor maintenance on emergency vehicles and clean them as needed.
Drive vehicles to transport materials and ensure safe operation according to regulations.
Inspect and maintain vehicle supplies and safety equipment, reporting mechanical problems.
Record vehicle routes and maintain logs of cargo and deliveries in accordance with regulations.
Report traffic or transportation situations using communication devices.
Provide advice and assistance to passengers regarding local areas and services.
Complete accident reports and record necessary information about incidents.
Examine freight to determine loading sequences and check specifications of materials loaded or unloaded against work orders.
Inform designated employees of items loaded or any problems encountered and transmit work orders to laborers.
Review work throughout the process to ensure proper performance and collaborate with workers and managers to solve work-related problems.
Plan work schedules and assign duties to maintain adequate staffing for effective performance of activities.
Supervise recycling technicians and community service workers, and conduct staff meetings to relay information or address safety topics.
Prepare and maintain records of employee time, wages, daily receipts, or inspection results, and record operating data.
Drive vehicles or operate machines to complete work assignments and assist workers, while ensuring equipment is cleaned and maintained.
Inspect or test materials, vehicles, and equipment for defects and compliance with specifications and safety standards.
Sort materials, such as metals, plastic, or glass, into appropriate containers for recycling, and maintain records of recycled materials.
Monitor fieldwork to ensure proper performance, plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet production goals.
